untranslatable_reason: Signal requires qualitative fundamental analysis ('fundamental analysis shows clear directional bias', 'market exhibits big fundamental potential as defined by news/events/structural changes', 'exit if fundamental premise weakens'). These are not quantifiable via the allowed technical indicators. The source text is also fragmented and does not provide clear quantitative entry/exit rules. Position sizing (2% risk per trade) and partial exit logic (33% at 2.5x risk with 1.5x ATR trailing) cannot be fully encoded in condition-based rules.
translation_notes: This signal is primarily a risk/position management framework built on fundamental analysis rather than technical indicators. The rule emphasizes: (1) conviction-based entry after fundamental confirmation, (2) letting profits run with a 2.5x extension factor, (3) accepting losses at 3.5% account threshold, (4) trailing stops using 1.5x ATR. While ATR-based trailing and fixed stop-loss are translatable, the core entry condition ('fundamental strength') and the exit condition ('fundamental premise weakens') require external data sources or manual discretion not available in the technical indicator set. A practical implementation would require integration with fundamental data feeds or manual signal generation.
